TopModule.demo_behavior:
Module: TopModule I would like you to implement a module named TopModule with the following interface. All input and output ports are one bit unless otherwise specified. - input  clk - input  a - input  b - output q - output state The module should implement a sequential circuit. The circuit consists of combinational logic and one bit of memory (i.e., one flip-flop). The output of the flip-flop has been made observable through the output state. Assume all sequential logic is triggered on the positive edge of the clock. Read the simulation waveforms to determine what the circuit does, then implement it. time   clk a   b   state q 0ns    0   1   x   x     x 5ns    1   1   x   x     x 10ns   0   0   0   x     x 15ns   1   0   0   0     0 20ns   0   0   0   0     0 25ns   1   0   0   0     0 30ns   0   0   0   0     0 35ns   1   0   0   0     0 40ns   0   0   0   0     0 45ns   1   0   1   0     1 50ns   0   0   1   0     1 55ns   1   1   0   0     1 60ns   0   1   0   0     1 65ns   1   1   1   0     0 70ns   0   1   1   0     0 75ns   1   0   0   1     1 80ns   0   0   0   1     1 85ns   1   1   1   0     0 90ns   0   1   1   0     0 95ns   1   1   1   1     1 100ns  0   1   1   1     1 105ns  1   1   1   1     1 110ns  0   1   1   1     1 115ns  1   1   0   1     0 120ns  0   1   0   1     0 125ns  1   0   1   1     0 130ns  0   0   1   1     0 135ns  1   0   0   1     1 140ns  0   0   0   1     1 145ns  1   0   0   0     0 150ns  0   0   0   0     0 155ns  1   0   0   0     0 160ns  0   0   0   0     0 165ns  1   0   0   0     0 170ns  0   0   0   0     0 175ns  1   0   0   0     0 180ns  0   0   0   0     0 185ns  1   0   0   0     0 190ns  0   0   0   0     0
Key rules: Implement behavior exactly as described in the prompt.
Reset: Only as explicitly described in the prompt.
Corner cases: No additional corner cases beyond prompt requirements.
Performance: Use prompt-defined timing/latency behavior.

test_goals: Pass benchmark harness checks for all provided tests.
verification.oracle_strategy: Use benchmark-provided reference outputs as oracle.
pass_fail_criteria: DUT outputs match oracle outputs for benchmark tests.
